channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Nine-Year-Old RefluXFS Linux Flaw Gives Local Users Root on Default RHEL Installs - The Hacker News
item.description:
1. Nine-Year-Old RefluXFS Linux Flaw Gives Local Users Root on Default RHEL Installs  The Hacker News
  2. RefluXFS: A Linux Kernel Local Privilege Escalation to Root in XFS (CVE-2026-64600)  Qualys
  3. Linux XFS has a decade-old race condition allowing full root access  Network World
  4. Linux Kernel Flaw Exposes 16 Million RHEL Systems to Silent Root Takeover  Tech Times
  5. Linux kernel flaw lets local users gain root access  SecurityBrief Australia

item.title: AMD EPYC 9006 Venice Announced & Looks Poised To Be A Grand Slam - Phoronix
item.description:
1. AMD EPYC 9006 Venice Announced & Looks Poised To Be A Grand Slam  Phoronix
  2. AMD’s 256-core Epyc 9996 ‘Venice’ claims up to a 3.4x jump over Intel Xeon competition, 20% over Nvidia Vera – Zen 6 comes with up to 1024MB of L3, 16-channel memory, and 5GHz+ clock speeds  Tom's Hardware
  3. AMD EPYC Zen 7 "Florence" Confirmed With ACE, Next-Gen Memory  Phoronix
  4. AMD EPYC Venice CPUs Stomp NVIDIA’s Vera With 20% Faster Single-Core & 2.2x Higher Throughput With Up to 256 “Zen 6” Cores, 203 Billion Transistors & Over 5 GHz+ Clocks  Wccftech
  5. Supermicro Introduces New Server Portfolio with 6th Gen AMD EPYC 9006 Series CPUs, Delivering 1.7x Generational Performance Improvements  TechPowerUp
